description: I would like to revisit our strategy around search. We've had several issues with trying to search mail natively: 1. Using applescript through the ClaraBridge mail integration requires iterating through every email and is prohibitively slow. 2. mdfind is fast, but is restricted from scanning ~/Library/Mail or any other application-specific data per Apple's privacy restrictions, which means it can't find emails or the default Obsidian notes vault location. If Obsidian is moved from the Obisidian iCloud folder into a regular iCloud folder, mdfind can search it just fine. 3. ripgrip or something similar can search the mail directory, but it maintains no index and will scale poorly and consume excess CPU 4. Our zk search is just doing a `strings.Contains(strings.Lower(string(body)))` which is fast enough, but lacks any specific zk markdown note awareness or indexing and could be improved. Minimally it could use mdfind. Here is what I propose for updating and unifying search: Our webex search relies on native webex search capability through the API and works well. This should be left as is. We should use mdfind for general file search exposing the `-onlyin` options through the MCP tool. For email, we should ingest these into sqlite using external content tables and then use FTS5 to index the content. This also allows us to potentially add email specific intelligence, such as parsing the `X-Universally-Unique-Identifier` and `In-Reply-To` headers to allow intelligent queries against this content. For zk, we should follow the same strategy as email and ingest the markdown files into sqlite and use FTS5 to index the content. This will allow us to potentially add zk specific intelligence when parsing the markdown files that may be helpful for search, such as recognizing backlinks, tags, etc. These will cost us some disk space for the indexes in our DB, but will be highly performant and gives us control over indexing app-specific content. It will also keep all of our search code unifed in our Go code base which I strongly prefer. I'm unsure about where to locate these tools. Should we just provide a "search" MCP server that provides both mdfind and sqlite based indexing? It would make more sense to exposed search through the various tools, e.g. zk search exposed through zk, which specifically indexes the folder defined in the configuration; general file search (mdfind) through the fs MCP server. I'm unsure about mail search in this model, though, as the mail implementation is exposed by ClaraBridge. Maybe an extension to the namespace mapping functionality. As none of this requires Apple's Swift libraries, it should all be implemented in Go, including the mail search. Please help me with designing and planning this and answering any questions to plan the implementation.
